Title: VqERF1B-VqERF062-VqNSTS2 transcriptional cascade enhances stilbene biosynthesis and resistance to powdery mildew in grapevine

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Vitis vinifera; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin activity and enhanced resistance to powdery mildew
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: stilbene branch competing with chalcone/flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms core stilbene products and commits flux toward stilbene branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-control enzyme for stilbene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: STS family; type III polyketide synthase conserved domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to produce resveratrol-type stilbenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes stilbene accumulation and enhances resistance to powdery mildew
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cytoplasm; upon infection accumulates around haustoria/haustorial encasement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: rapidly induced in Danfeng-2 leaves after artificial inoculation with Erysiphe necator, then declines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: detectable in transgenic Arabidopsis and grapevine leaves; accumulates at fungal invasion sites after inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in tobacco with metabolite detection; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis and grapevine; RNAi knockdown in grape leaves; HPLC metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation assays; subcellular localization/confocal imaging
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: VqERF062; VqERF1B (transcriptional regulators upstream, not direct enzyme partners)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: artificial inoculation with Erysiphe necator or Golovinomyces cichoracearum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProVqNSTS2 (native, inducible/pathogen responsive) in Arabidopsis; 35S constitutive in grapevine overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising gene for engineering grape powdery mildew resistance and stilbene enrichment, potentially useful alone or stacked with upstream ERF regulators
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transient expression in tobacco and stable expression in Arabidopsis demonstrated de novo stilbene production in species lacking endogenous STS genes. Because of high sequence similarity, RNAi in Danfeng-2 simultaneously targeted VqNSTS2 and VqSTS37.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VqNSTS2 encodes a bona fide stilbene synthase with conserved STS motifs and cytoplasmic localization. 2. Its transient expression in tobacco produced stilbenes, demonstrating enzymatic activity in a heterologous system lacking STS. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis and Thompson Seedless increased trans-resveratrol/piceid (and ε-viniferin in grapevine) and enhanced resistance to powdery mildew. 4. After pathogen challenge, VqNSTS2 protein accumulated at infection sites and wrapped fungal haustoria, forming encasement-like barriers. 5. RNAi suppression reduced stilbenes and disease resistance, supporting a positive causal role in stilbene metabolism and defense.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates VqNSTS2 by binding DRE and RAA motifs in its promoter; directly activates VqERF1B by binding GCC-box in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback/feedforward loop with VqERF1B; VqERF062 activates VqERF1B and also forms a protein complex with VqERF1B to further enhance VqNSTS2 expression; VqERF062 also forms homodimers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms heteromeric complex with VqERF1B and homodimer with itself
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases VqNSTS2 expression, stilbene accumulation, and powdery mildew resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct upstream activator and major positive regulator of the stilbene pathway through VqNSTS2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: responsive to powdery mildew and peaks at 4 dpi in Danfeng-2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in overexpression grapevine lines; reduced in RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; dual-luciferase assays; Y1H; EMSA; transient overexpression; stable overexpression and RNAi in grapevine; HPLC metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; FRET-AB
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive for overexpression; promoter-LUC reporters in tobacco transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq PRJNA306731 used for co-expression screening
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lines showed reduced disease resistance; no growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate regulator for breeding or metabolic engineering to elevate stilbenes and powdery mildew resistance; useful for stacking with VqNSTS2 or VqERF1B
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PAL and CHS also changed in transgenic lines, STS expression showed the clearest directional correlation with stilbene accumulation, supporting VqNSTS2 as the principal direct metabolic target.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VqERF062 was identified by RNA-seq co-expression and dual-luciferase screening as a strong activator of the VqNSTS2 promoter. 2. It binds directly to DRE and RAA motifs in the VqNSTS2 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Transient/stable overexpression increased VqNSTS2 transcript abundance, stilbene accumulation, and resistance to E. necator, whereas RNAi had opposite effects on defense and STS expression. 4. VqERF062 physically interacts with VqERF1B and this complex enhances VqNSTS2 promoter activation beyond VqERF062 alone. 5. VqERF062 also binds the GCC-box in the VqERF1B promoter, establishing a positive transcriptional cascade/feedback module.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: positive regulation of stilbene accumulation via transcriptional cascade with VqERF062 and promotion of VqNSTS2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enhances VqERF062-mediated activation of VqNSTS2 promoter; itself is transcriptionally activated by VqERF062
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated by VqERF062; associated with powdery mildew response
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in positive feedback/feedforward loop with VqERF062
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with VqERF062 in vivo and in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes stilbene accumulation and enhances powdery mildew resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive co-regulator that amplifies VqNSTS2 transcription and downstream stilbene production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transient overexpression increased transcript levels ~2.3-fold in Danfeng-2 leaves; RNAi reduced expression by ~50%
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; FRET-AB; dual-luciferase assays; transient overexpression and RNAi in grape leaves; HPLC metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for interaction/effector assays; transient OE and RNAi constructs in grapevine leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful co-regulatory target for strengthening stilbene biosynthesis and powdery mildew resistance, especially in combination with VqERF062 and/or VqNSTS2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper demonstrates VqERF1B as a positive regulator mainly through interaction with VqERF062 and transient functional assays rather than direct DNA-binding to VqNSTS2 promoter on its own.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VqERF1B was identified as a VqERF062-interacting ERF transcription factor. 2. Physical interaction with VqERF062 was validated by Y2H, BiFC, pull-down, and FRET-AB. 3. Co-expression of VqERF1B with VqERF062 enhanced VqNSTS2 promoter activation more strongly than VqERF062 alone. 4. Transient overexpression of VqERF1B increased resveratrol, piceid, and ε-viniferin, while RNAi reduced these stilbenes. 5. VqERF1B overexpression restricted powdery mildew growth, whereas RNAi increased susceptibility, indicating a positive regulatory role in stilbene-associated defense.
